news.title: Drain-top buildings to face higher tax in Sambhajinagar; crackdown on direct sewage discharge
news.para: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar municipal corporation will raise property taxes for buildings constructed over major drains. This move aims to tackle monsoon flooding and improve desilting operations. The civic body also ordered strict action against direct sewage discharge into stormwater drains. Enforcement drives will ensure properties connect to sewage treatment plants.

news.title: Delhi to overhaul property registration system, modernise sub-registrar offices: CM
news.para: The Delhi government is preparing to completely overhaul the property registration system in the capital, aiming to make Sub-Registrar offices more modern, transparent and citizen-friendly, with a focus on eliminating delays, corruption and dependence on agents.

news.title: Lucknow development body to redevelop dilapidated complex in Indira Nagar at ₹110 crore
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is set to redevelop the 40-year-old Kailashkunj Commercial Complex into a 13-storey mixed-use high-rise. The project, estimated at Rs 110 crore, will feature commercial spaces on lower floors and 3BHK residential flats above. Allottees will receive 1.25 times their current area in the new modern complex.

news.title: Chandigarh plans vertical shift under master plan 2031
news.para: Chandigarh's Master Plan 2031 proposes significant changes, allowing higher density, taller buildings, and greater design flexibility.  These amendments aim for vertical growth and optimized land use, contrasting with previous restrictive norms.  The changes include increased FAR, relaxed height restrictions, and adjusted ground coverage across various land-use categories.

news.title: Illegal sand mining under lens as Chhattisgarh government steps up seizure drive
news.para: Chhattisgarh has intensified its crackdown on illegal sand mining with a recent seizure of heavy machinery and vehicles along the Jonk river. The state has also launched a toll-free helpline, 1800-233-2140, to empower citizens to report mining-related irregularities and strengthen surveillance across the state.

news.title: Jharkhand building regularisation rules' amendment sought by technical professionals
news.para: Architects and engineers in Jharkhand are urging the Chief Minister to change new building regularization rules. Current rules exclude many people due to plot size limits. They propose using built-up area instead of total plot size. Road width requirements also prevent many older homes from qualifying. These changes aim to help more residents regularize their constructions.

news.title: Jaipur: Ramganj building owner asked to raze tilted structure in 48 hour
news.para: A five-storey building in Jaipur's Ramganj has been declared unsafe and issued a 48-hour demolition notice by the Municipal Corporation. Residents had expressed panic and fear due to the visibly tilted structure, leading to vacated nearby houses and restricted movement. The corporation warned of demolition and cost recovery if the owner fails to act.

news.title: Fly ash continues to haunt Ennore residents
news.para: While the NGT had ordered the removal of fly ash deposited along the creek  river bed and the flood plains, roughly amounting to 56.7 lakh metric tonnes, and to plug the leaks in all pipelines that carry the ash slurry, a visit by TOI to Ennore and nearby hamlets revealed that the compliance by Tangedco has been subpar at best

news.title: UDH notification sparks fear over commercialisation of residential areas in Jaipur
news.para: Jaipur's Urban Development and Housing department has permitted many commercial activities in residential areas. This decision sparks worry among planners and officials. They fear it will alter neighborhood character, increase congestion, and strain civic infrastructure. The move could lead to hotels and shopping complexes in residential lanes. This raises concerns about traffic, pollution, and essential services.

news.title: ED arrests Punjab real estate businessman in land fraud case
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has arrested real estate businessman Ajay Sehgal in a money laundering probe linked to alleged fake consent letters for Punjab government's Change of Land Use (CLU). Sehgal, Secretary of ICHBS, is accused of forging signatures for the Suntec City project and other developments, with sales exceeding Rs 200 crore. Investigations into other officials are ongoing.

news.title: DMRC eyes December rollout for key phase-IV corridors, fixes target for phase-V expansion
news.para: Delhi Metro is set to inaugurate three new corridors by December, expanding the Magenta and Pink Lines, and introducing the Aerocity-Tughlakabad route. These additions are part of Phase 4, with further expansions under Phase 4 and proposed Phase 5 projects aiming to enhance connectivity across underserved areas of the National Capital Region.

news.title: Maharashtra: TDR emerges as new age currency for infrastructure works
news.para: Besa-Pipla Nagar Panchayat is piloting a TDR-based infrastructure model, sparking debate on its potential as a new currency for civic development. This innovative approach allows developers to fund projects like roads and drainage in exchange for development rights, reducing direct financial outlay for local authorities and offering a path to accelerate growth amid funding constraints.

news.title: Maharashtra to issue free rural property cards; sanad fees waived
news.para: Maharashtra government will provide free property cards and waive sanad fees for rural residents under the Centre's Swamitva scheme. This initiative aims to simplify ownership rights, reduce property disputes, and accelerate the settlement of property records across the state. The decision follows extensive digital land mapping and property measurement exercises.

news.title: Bengaluru owners left high and dry after rental promises fall flat
news.para: Property owners are reporting significant delays in receiving rental income from Jones Asset Management, with some facing non-payment for up to eight months. These issues have led to disputes over agreements and concerns about transparency. The situation highlights a growing problem, prompting warnings for both owners and tenants to exercise caution with intermediaries.

news.title: Hyderabad's western corridor may see spike in property registration rates
news.para: Telangana government is considering a significant increase in property registration rates, potentially quadrupling them in Hyderabad's upscale western corridor. Proposed revisions in areas like Raidurg and Gachibowli could see rates jump from around Rs 13 crore to Rs 55 crore per acre. This move aims to bridge the gap between market prices and current registration values, potentially boosting state revenue.

news.title: Over 110 developers get Odisha RERA notices over missing progress reports
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Regulatory Authority (Orera) has issued show-cause notices to 113 developers for failing to submit mandatory quarterly progress reports. Non-compliance, a violation of Rera provisions, can lead to penalties up to 5% of project cost, formal inquiries, and potential suspension or revocation of registration.

news.title: HDFC Bank backs Hyderabad luxury housing project with ₹300 crore
news.para: HDFC Bank has provided Rs 300 crore in funding for a Rs 2,000 crore ultra-luxury residential project in Hyderabad's Kokapet. This investment highlights a trend of developers seeking structured financing amid moderating sales and strong demand for premium homes, with Hyderabad leading southern India's luxury market.

news.title: Dalmia Bharat to acquire JAL's cement assets in ₹2,850 crore deal after Adani takeover
news.para: Dalmia Bharat's subsidiary will acquire cement assets from debt-ridden Jaiprakash Associates for Rs 2,850 crore. This strategic acquisition will boost Dalmia Bharat's cement capacity to 54.7 MTPA, with further expansion planned. The deal, expected to finalize within two weeks, aims to diversify markets and reduce regional volatility.

news.title: Kolte-Patil Developers posts net loss ₹38.08 crore in FY26
news.para: Kolte-Patil Developers reported a net consolidated loss of ₹38.08 crore for FY26, a significant downturn from a profit of ₹109.33 crore in FY25. The company's total income also saw a substantial decline of 54.50% to ₹802.50 crore. The board has approved a scheme of amalgamation for its wholly-owned subsidiaries.

news.title: Century Plyboards' net profit rises 44.20% to ₹268.33 crore in FY26
news.para: Century Plyboards reported a significant 44.20% growth in net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹268.33 crore. The company also announced plans to expand its MDF and particle board capacities, investing substantially over the next few years to meet rising demand and improve operational efficiency.
